source_paper_title: Indefinite Linear-Quadratic Partially Observed Mean-Field Game
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Chen_2025, title={Indefinite Linear-Quadratic Partially Observed Mean-Field Game}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2024.0748}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2024.0748}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Chen, Tian and Nie, Tianyang and Wu, Zhen}, year={2025}, month=dec }
context:
Page 14, Remark 4.13(ii) (following Theorem 4.12, Section 4.3 "Feedback representation").

Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still a problem.
title: Solvability conditions for asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation in mean-field game feedback
background_latex:
Consider a continuous-time linear-quadratic (LQ) mean-field game with common noise and partial observation, in which the decentralized optimal control is expressed in linear state-feedback form once certain matrix-valued Riccati equations are solvable.

Let the state dimension be 
\(n\) and the control dimension be \(m\). Let \(A_t,\bar A_t,D_t,\bar D_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\), \(B_t,\bar B_t,F_t,\bar F_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), \(Q_t\in\mathbb{S}^n\), \(R_t\in\mathbb{S}^m\), \(S_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), and \(L_T\in\mathbb{S}^n\) be given deterministic coefficient functions/matrices on \([0,T]\) (bounded/measurable as needed so the equations below are well-defined). The scalar constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_3,\beta_1\) (and other constants appearing in the model) are given.

In the paper’s feedback derivation, two Riccati-type ordinary differential equations appear. One is a (symmetric) stochastic-LQ Riccati equation for a symmetric matrix \(\Pi_t\in\mathbb{S}^n\). The other is a generally \emph{asymmetric} (not necessarily symmetric) Riccati equation for a matrix \(\Sigma_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) of the form
\[
\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^\top \Sigma_t + D_t^\top \Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t)
- \Sigma_{e,t} R_{e,t}^{-1} \bar\Sigma_t^\top + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,
\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T.
\]
Here the auxiliary matrices \(R_{e,t}\in\mathbb{R}^{m\times m}\), \(\Sigma_{e,t}\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), and \(\bar\Sigma_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\) are (as defined in the paper) affine functions of \(R_t,S_t,F_t,\bar F_t,B_t,\bar B_t\) and \(\Sigma_t\); in particular \(R_{e,t}\) depends on \(\Sigma_t\) and must be (uniformly) invertible for the equation to make sense.

This Riccati equation is called in the paper an “asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure,” reflecting that neither \(Q_t\) nor \(R_t\) is assumed positive semidefinite/definite, and \(\Sigma_t\) is not restricted to be symmetric.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine general verifiable conditions on the coefficient data \(A_t,\bar A_t,D_t,\bar D_t,B_t,\bar B_t,F_t,\bar F_t,Q_t,R_t,S_t,L_T\) and constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_3,\beta_1\) that guarantee existence and uniqueness of a (sufficiently regular) solution \(\Sigma:[0,T]\to\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) to the (generally asymmetric) indefinite Riccati equation
\[
\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^\top \Sigma_t + D_t^\top \Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t)
- \Sigma_{e,t} R_{e,t}^{-1} \bar\Sigma_t^\top + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,
\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T,
\]
in a way that ensures \(R_{e,t}\) is (uniformly) invertible on \([0,T]\) (so that \(R_{e,t}^{-1}\) is well-defined) and the resulting feedback representation for decentralized strategies is well-posed.
latex:
Consider a continuous-time linear-quadratic (LQ) mean-field game with common noise and partial observation, in which the decentralized optimal control is expressed in linear state-feedback form once certain matrix-valued Riccati equations are solvable.

Let the state dimension be 
\(n\) and the control dimension be \(m\). Let \(A_t,\bar A_t,D_t,\bar D_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\), \(B_t,\bar B_t,F_t,\bar F_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), \(Q_t\in\mathbb{S}^n\), \(R_t\in\mathbb{S}^m\), \(S_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), and \(L_T\in\mathbb{S}^n\) be given deterministic coefficient functions/matrices on \([0,T]\) (bounded/measurable as needed so the equations below are well-defined). The scalar constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_3,\beta_1\) (and other constants appearing in the model) are given.

In the paper’s feedback derivation, two Riccati-type ordinary differential equations appear. One is a (symmetric) stochastic-LQ Riccati equation for a symmetric matrix \(\Pi_t\in\mathbb{S}^n\). The other is a generally \emph{asymmetric} (not necessarily symmetric) Riccati equation for a matrix \(\Sigma_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) of the form
\[
\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^\top \Sigma_t + D_t^\top \Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t)
- \Sigma_{e,t} R_{e,t}^{-1} \bar\Sigma_t^\top + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,
\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T.
\]
Here the auxiliary matrices \(R_{e,t}\in\mathbb{R}^{m\times m}\), \(\Sigma_{e,t}\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\), and \(\bar\Sigma_t\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times m}\) are (as defined in the paper) affine functions of \(R_t,S_t,F_t,\bar F_t,B_t,\bar B_t\) and \(\Sigma_t\); in particular \(R_{e,t}\) depends on \(\Sigma_t\) and must be (uniformly) invertible for the equation to make sense.

This Riccati equation is called in the paper an “asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure,” reflecting that neither \(Q_t\) nor \(R_t\) is assumed positive semidefinite/definite, and \(\Sigma_t\) is not restricted to be symmetric.

Determine general verifiable conditions on the coefficient data \(A_t,\bar A_t,D_t,\bar D_t,B_t,\bar B_t,F_t,\bar F_t,Q_t,R_t,S_t,L_T\) and constants \(\alpha_1,\alpha_3,\beta_1\) that guarantee existence and uniqueness of a (sufficiently regular) solution \(\Sigma:[0,T]\to\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}\) to the (generally asymmetric) indefinite Riccati equation
\[
\dot\Sigma_t + \Sigma_t(A_t+\bar A_t) + A_t^\top \Sigma_t + D_t^\top \Sigma_t(D_t+\bar D_t)
- \Sigma_{e,t} R_{e,t}^{-1} \bar\Sigma_t^\top + (1-\alpha_1)Q_t = 0,
\qquad \Sigma_T = (1-\alpha_3)L_T,
\]
in a way that ensures \(R_{e,t}\) is (uniformly) invertible on \([0,T]\) (so that \(R_{e,t}^{-1}\) is well-defined) and the resulting feedback representation for decentralized strategies is well-posed.
open_problem_quote: Noticing that Riccati equation (35) is an asymmetric indefinite equation with complex structure, its solvability is still a problem.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The question asks for general existence/uniqueness conditions for a nonlinear matrix ODE with invertibility constraints; falsifying a proposed condition by finite-dimensional search is possible but not clearly aligned with the paper’s open-ended formulation, which does not specify a conjectured criterion to refute.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: An evolutionary loop could explore coefficient families and numerically integrate the Riccati ODE to detect blow-up or loss of invertibility of \(R_{e,t}\), helping hypothesize sufficient conditions or identify stabilizing structural restrictions. However, the target is a general theorem-level characterization, and numerical fitness signals would only provide heuristic guidance rather than a definitive resolution.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
In Chen–Nie–Wu (2025), the feedback form of decentralized strategies for an indefinite partially observed mean-field game with common noise hinges on two matrix ODEs: a symmetric indefinite stochastic-LQ Riccati equation for \(\Pi_t\) and a generally asymmetric, indefinite Riccati equation for \(\Sigma_t\) (their equation (35)). The paper resolves well-posedness of the Hamiltonian FBSDE and the symmetric Riccati \(\Pi_t\) by introducing a relaxed compensator \(P\) (an inequality/LMI-type condition (RC)) inspired by equivalent cost functional methods. However, it explicitly leaves open general verifiable conditions ensuring existence/uniqueness of \(\Sigma_t\) together with uniform invertibility of the effective control weight \(R_{e,t}\), noting that \(\Sigma_t\) is not constrained to be symmetric and \(R_{e,t}\) depends on \(\Sigma_t\).

The closest available theory comes from indefinite stochastic LQ control and generalized Riccati equations (Chen–Li–Zhou 1998; Chen–Zhou 2000; Rami–Moore–Zhou 2002) and from the equivalent cost functional/convexity approach (Yu 2013; Sun–Li–Yong 2016; Sun–Xiong–Yong 2021). These works provide verifiable sufficient conditions for symmetric Riccati solvability and uniform convexity (or definiteness after compensation), and they clarify how invertibility of effective control weights like \(R_t+F_t^\top \Pi_t F_t\) can be guaranteed. Mean-field extensions in indefinite settings (Li–Li–Yu 2020) indicate that mean-field coupling typically leads to coupled Riccati systems, but still largely within symmetric frameworks. Partial observation MFG references (Bensoussan–Feng–Huang 2021; Chen–Du–Wu 2024) supply the filtering/backward separation scaffolding but assume positive-definite weights, avoiding the asymmetric indefinite Riccati phenomenon.

At present, there is no cited work that settles the asymmetric indefinite Riccati equation (35) in the generality posed (time-varying coefficients, \(\Sigma_t\) nonsymmetric, and \(R_{e,t}\) depending on \(\Sigma_t\)). A promising direction suggested by the surrounding literature is to seek a transformation that embeds (35) into a higher-dimensional symmetric generalized Riccati equation or an LMI/compensator condition analogous to (RC), thereby yielding a priori bounds ensuring \(R_{e,t}\) remains uniformly invertible. Another direction is to identify structural regimes (commutativity/simultaneous triangularization, small coupling \(\bar A,\bar B,\bar D,\bar F\), or symmetry-restoring parameter constraints such as \(\bar A=\delta I\), \(\bar B=\bar D=\bar F=0\), \(\alpha_2=\beta_2\)) under which (35) reduces to a symmetric indefinite Riccati equation, for which existing indefinite SLQ theory applies.

sources.title: Stochastic linear quadratic regulators with indefinite control weight costs
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces solvability theory for stochastic LQ control with indefinite control weight via generalized Riccati equations and conditions ensuring well-posedness.
sources.connection: Provides foundational indefinite SLQ Riccati machinery that underlies the symmetric Riccati equation for \(\Pi_t\) and informs what might be needed for the asymmetric mean-field Riccati \(\Sigma_t\), though it does not treat asymmetry or mean-field coupling.

sources.title: Stochastic linear quadratic regulators with indefinite control weight costs II
sources.theorem_or_result: Extends indefinite SLQ theory, including solvability and structure of associated Riccati equations in stochastic settings.
sources.connection: Further background on indefinite Riccati solvability; relevant as the mean-field game feedback derivation relies on indefinite quadratic costs, but it does not address the asymmetric Riccati structure induced by mean-field coupling and partial observation.

sources.title: Indefinite stochastic linear quadratic control and generalized differential Riccati equation
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops generalized Riccati equation framework for indefinite stochastic LQ control, giving conditions for existence/uniqueness and links to optimal control.
sources.connection: Closest classical source for indefinite Riccati solvability; suggests that generalized Riccati/linear matrix inequality conditions may be a route to verifiable conditions for \(R_{e,t}\) invertibility and \(\Sigma_t\) existence, but does not cover asymmetric mean-field Riccati equations.

sources.title: Equivalent cost functionals and stochastic linear quadratic optimal control problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces equivalent cost functional transformations that can convert indefinite SLQ problems into definite ones under suitable compensator constructions.
sources.connection: Directly motivates the relaxed compensator method used in the source paper to guarantee solvability of the symmetric Riccati \(\Pi_t\); analogous transformations might help recast the asymmetric \(\Sigma_t\) equation or derive sufficient conditions for \(R_{e,t}\) invertibility.

sources.title: Open-loop and closed-loop solvabilities for stochastic linear quadratic optimal control problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes equivalences between uniform convexity, Riccati solvability, and closed-loop solvability for (possibly indefinite) stochastic LQ problems.
sources.connection: The source paper uses this equivalence (via relaxed compensator) for \(\Pi_t\). Extending these ideas to the asymmetric mean-field Riccati \(\Sigma_t\) would require new arguments because \(\Sigma_t\) is not symmetric and the quadratic form structure is altered by mean-field coupling.

sources.title: Indefinite stochastic linear-quadratic optimal control problems with random coefficients: Closed-loop representation of open-loop optimal controls
sources.theorem_or_result: Provides closed-loop representations and solvability results for indefinite SLQ with random coefficients, refining the Riccati-based framework.
sources.connection: Relevant for understanding robustness of Riccati solvability under randomness; however the open problem concerns a deterministic-coefficient but asymmetric Riccati ODE arising from mean-field game coupling.

sources.title: Indefinite mean-field type linear-quadratic stochastic optimal control problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies indefinite mean-field type LQ stochastic control, deriving Riccati systems and solvability under generalized conditions.
sources.connection: Closer to mean-field coupling than classical SLQ. While it treats mean-field type control (not games) and typically symmetric Riccati systems, it informs what coefficient conditions might ensure well-posedness and invertibility of effective control weights analogous to \(R_{e,t}\).

sources.title: Linear-quadratic-Gaussian mean-field-game with partial observation and common noise
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops LQG mean-field games with partial observation and common noise under positive-definite conditions, using filtering and Riccati equations.
sources.connection: Provides the closest partial-observation/common-noise MFG template; however it relies on definiteness and symmetric Riccati equations, whereas the open problem is the asymmetric indefinite Riccati \(\Sigma_t\) governing mean-field feedback.

sources.title: Partially observed mean-field game and related mean-field forward-backward stochastic differential equation
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies partially observed mean-field games via backward separation and mean-field FBSDEs under positive-definite settings.
sources.connection: Methodological precursor for the backward separation approach in the source paper; does not address indefinite costs or the asymmetric Riccati equation \(\Sigma_t\) with \(R_{e,t}\) depending on \(\Sigma_t\).
